strategic_profile.executive_summary: Gonzaga University is a private, mid-sized Jesuit institution known for its strong community, high retention rate (93%), and competitive direct-admit nursing program. Emphasizing a holistic, mission-driven education, the university boasts excellent post-graduate outcomes with alumni securing median 10-year earnings of $80,896.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Nursing is heavily impacted and requires Direct Admission.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants interested in healthcare must commit to the November 15 Early Action deadline, as internal transfers into nursing are prohibited.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The waitlist admit rate hovers between 24% and 27%.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Unlike Ivy League institutions, waitlisted applicants at Gonzaga have a statistically viable chance of eventual admission if they opt-in and show continued interest.

analyst_takeaways.insight: 78% of students participate in research or internships, and the campus has a massive intramural and varsity sports culture.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students looking for a balance of collaborative academics, strong athletics (Pac-12 bound), and outdoor recreation will fit the institutional archetype.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A collaborative, tight-knit campus deeply rooted in Jesuit traditions of service. Strong emphasis on intramural sports, outdoor activities (hiking, skiing), and Division I basketball (the 'Kennel').
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located a half-mile from downtown Spokane with access to an international airport and rideshares, though out-of-state students may find the inland Northwest winters and distance from coastal cities isolating.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Despite a primarily undergraduate focus, 78% of students engage in academic research, real-world projects, or internships.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional indefinitely. Homeschooled students with fewer than 30 college credits and international students requiring English proficiency must submit SAT or ACT scores.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admit rate calculated based on the number of students who accepted their spot on the waitlist.

gpa.notes: 3.76 is the reported median GPA for the enrolled Fall 2024 freshman class.

testing.policy_note: Test-optional policy artificially inflates reported score averages, as only competitive scorers submit.

colleges.name: School of Health Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students highly committed to a four-year nursing track who possess rigorous high school science and math backgrounds.

majors.name: Nursing
prerequisite_courses: 4 years of high school Natural/Physical Laboratory Science
majors.special_requirements: Requires an additional 800-word supplemental essay on motivations for nursing.
majors.notes: Direct admission only. Students may not switch into the nursing program from another major once enrolled at the university.

colleges.name: School of Business Administration
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students seeking a blend of Jesuit ethical education and practical, globally-focused corporate training.

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.notes: Requires a minimum 2.0 GPA across the six lower-division business core courses to progress.

colleges.name: College of Arts & Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students interested in a broad liberal arts foundation integrated with undergraduate research.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.special_requirements: Must earn a B- or better in PSYC 206 to complete the major.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires one primary short-answer essay (300 words) focusing on community impact or Jesuit values.
Requires an additional 800-word essay explaining the applicant's choice to pursue nursing.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Interviews are highly recommended for any student applying with an unweighted GPA below 3.2 or an SAT below 1150.

major_selection_tactics: Apply Early Action for Nursing, as it is the only pathway into the program.
If undecided but interested in STEM, apply to Engineering; internal transfers into Engineering later can delay graduation.
Use the interview to add context to a lower GPA, as the university explicitly flags sub-3.2 applicants as prime interview candidates.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Gonzaga allows alternate majors, but direct-admit Nursing strictly denies internal transfer requests. Students interested in healthcare but lacking a highly competitive profile may consider applying to Biology or Health Sciences as an alternate.

scholarships.name: Regents Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: High-value merit scholarship recognizing elite academic achievement.

scholarships.name: Trustees Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Merit aid based on GPA and course rigor.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 4 or 5 are required to earn course credit.
ap_policy.usage: A 4 grants introductory credit, while a 5 may fulfill specific major requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Scores of 5 or higher on Higher Level exams yield credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students transferring with approved Associate degrees (AA/AS) can waive significant portions of the University Core.

what_it_takes: A demonstrated commitment to social justice and community service.
Strong academic rigor, specifically math and science for STEM/Nursing.
A desire for a collaborative rather than cutthroat academic environment.
common_activities: Outdoor recreation (Skiing at Mt. Spokane, hiking)
essay_tips: Highlight genuine, long-term commitments to community service.
Showcase an understanding of 'Cura Personalis' (care for the whole person).
red_flags: A purely transactional approach to academics without community involvement.
insights: Spokane's location offers great outdoor life but can feel isolating for students seeking major metropolitan internships without traveling.
